items.quote: Born among students in the early 1900s. Pre-war students created it. Post-war students kept it going.
items.name: Origin

items.quote: You declare how many tricks you'll win, then you have to deliver. Miss your bid and you go negative. That gap between confidence and result is what makes people come back.
items.name: The core loop

items.quote: The rules haven't changed. The medium has. Full trick-taking mechanics, browser-based, no download.

items.question: What is Meisen Trump?
items.answer: A 4-player, 2-team trick-taking card game from Kyushu Institute of Technology. It centers on bidding—declaring how many tricks you'll win and which suit is trump—and has been passed down through student clubs for over 100 years.

items.question: Can I play alone?
items.answer: Yes. Add a COM player and the AI fills the empty seat. It's useful for practicing before playing against real opponents, or when you can't get four people together.

items.question: Is it hard to learn?
items.answer: The rules have real depth, but most players pick it up quickly by jumping in with COM. Most are ready for their first real game within minutes.

items.question: How many players?
items.answer: 4 players in 2 teams. Partners sit opposite each other. If you can't fill all four seats, COM players take the empty spots.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Completely free. Create an account and start playing. No app to install—works in any browser.

overview.title: Game Overview
overview.description: Meitra is a strategic 4-player, 2-team card game. Players make bids based on their hands and decide how to help their team gain an advantage or avoid losing ground. The rules are simple, but the decisions are deep.
overview.objectiveDesc: Achieve the number of pairs declared in the bid, and the first team to reach the target score wins.
overview.objectiveExample: This is a team game. The two players sitting opposite each other form a team, and the target score can be changed in the room settings.
overview.gameFlowDesc: Create an account, then create a room from the room list page. You can set the target score when creating the room. A COM player is included by default, so you can start a solo game immediately. If a friend joins the room, the COM player is automatically replaced by that friend.
overview.gameFlowExample: Meitra is a trick-taking game. Each round proceeds in the order Deal -> Bid -> Trick. Trick is played 10 times, once for each card in hand. If a team Takes at least as many Tricks as it Bid, that team earns points. Rounds continue until one team reaches the target total score first.

cards.title: Cards and Deck
cards.compositionDesc: Cards used for play: 5-A plus JOKER (53 cards total). 2, 3, and 4 are excluded.
cards.compositionExample: Each suit (♠♥♦♣): 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, J, Q, K, A + 1 JOKER
cards.strengthDesc: Each card has a strength value, and higher numbers are stronger. The basic strength order is JOKER(150) > A(14) > K(13) > Q(12) > J(11) > 10(10) > 9(9) > 8(8) > 7(7) > 6(6) > 5(5).
cards.strengthExample: During play, this base strength gets a +100 bonus for trump cards and a +50 bonus for the base suit.

blow.title: Bidding System
blow.description: In Bid, you declare 'how many pairs out of 10 fields you can take' and 'which suit to use as trump.' This system is the core of the game. A pair means the number of fields (tricks) you can win.
blow.basicRuleDesc: A minimum bid of 6 pairs is required. You declare a combination of pair count and suit.
blow.hierarchyNote: With the same number of pairs, the declaration with the stronger trump suit takes priority.
blow.higherDeclarationDesc: ① Increase number of pairs ② Use stronger suit with same pairs
blow.higherDeclarationExample: Club 7 → Heart 7 (same pairs, stronger suit) / Club 8 (more pairs)
blow.practiceDesc: Experience the actual bidding system and understand the higher declaration rules.

jack.title: JOKER and Jack Special System
jack.description: JOKER is always the strongest card. Jacks (J) also have a special role. After the trump suit is determined, certain Jacks gain special strength.
jack.rulesIntro: There are special rules for card strength:
jack.jokerDesc: Always the strongest card in any situation (strength 150)
jack.mainJackDesc: The Jack of the trump suit (strength 19)
jack.subJackDesc: The Jack of the same color as the trump suit (strength 18)
jack.traExceptionDesc: The Main J/Sub J system does not apply to Tra declarations. All Jacks are treated with normal strength.

play.title: Play Phase
play.description: After bidding ends, players actually play cards and compete for fields (tricks). The round is decided by how many pairs can be taken across 10 fields.
play.orderDesc: Cards are played from the dealer (highest declarer) in seat order
play.orderExample: 1st: Dealer -> 2nd and later: one player at a time in seat order
play.winnerDesc: Based on the card strength system, the player who played the strongest card wins.
play.baseSuitDesc: The suit of the first card played becomes the base suit for that field.
play.baseSuitExample: ♠5 played first → ♠ is base suit → ♠ cards get +50 bonus
play.strengthDesc: Base strength + Trump bonus (+100) + Base suit bonus (+50)

broken.title: Broken & Negri System
broken.description: Under certain hand conditions, you enter a 'Broken' state and special rules apply. Choosing Negri, the weakest or least useful card, is also an important element.
broken.judgmentDesc: A hand is Broken when it has no face cards (A, K, Q, J), or only a single Queen.
broken.requiredDesc: If you hold all four Jacks, the game automatically treats the hand as Required Broken
broken.requiredExample: Holding ♠J, ♥J, ♦J, ♣J -> automatically handled as Required Broken
broken.negriDesc: Each player selects the card they consider least useful in their hand.

scoring.title: Scoring System
scoring.description: Score is calculated based on the number of pairs declared and the number of fields actually won.
scoring.calculatorDesc: Enter declared pairs and fields won to calculate the actual score.
scoring.formulaDesc: Let X be the declared number of pairs, and Y the number of fields won.
scoring.formulaExample: If Y≥X: 0.5×(Y-X)+X-5 points / If Y'<'X: Y-X points
scoring.tip1: Safe declarations (6-7 pairs) have lower failure risk
scoring.tip2: Bonus for exceeding declaration is modest (0.5×)
scoring.tip3: Penalty for failed declaration is severe (full difference)
scoring.tip4: High declarations have higher base points but greater risk

chombo.title: Chombo (Violation) System [Not Implemented]
chombo.description: Chombo (violation) rules are not implemented in the current version of Meitra. The items below describe rules planned for a future implementation.
chombo.negriForgetDesc: A violation for forgetting to choose a Negri card.
chombo.negriForgetExample: Negri not selected at play start → Can be reported as violation
chombo.lastTanzenDesc: A special violation when the last card in hand is JOKER.
chombo.lastTanzenExample: Last card in hand is JOKER → Processed as special violation
chombo.wrongBrokenDesc: A violation for declaring Broken when the hand is not actually Broken.
chombo.wrongBrokenExample: Declaring Broken with face cards → Can be reported as violation
chombo.reportSystemDesc: Only the opposing team can point out violations; not your own team.
chombo.reportSystemExample: Violation detected → Opposing team reports → Penalty applied

strategy.title: Strategy and Tips
strategy.blowTip1: Judge the strength of your hand and estimate how many pairs you can take out of 10 fields.
strategy.blowTip2: Stronger trump suits make a bid more powerful, but also increase the risk.
strategy.blowTip3: Start with safer bids around 6-7 pairs.
strategy.blowTip4: Watch your partner's bidding pattern and coordinate around it.
strategy.playTip1: Trump cards are powerful, but timing matters.
strategy.playTip2: Pay attention to the base suit when playing cards.
strategy.playTip3: Main J and Sub J are among the strongest cards. Decide whether to save them or use them early.
strategy.playTip4: Try to make use of your partner's strong cards.
strategy.playTip5: Remember the opponents' declarations and adjust which fields you let them take.
strategy.teamTip1: Play in ways that support your partner's bid.
strategy.teamTip2: It is also important to disrupt the opposing team's bid.
strategy.teamTip4: Share score awareness so your team understands the game state.
strategy.teamTip5: Keep the room target score in mind over the long game

reference.title: Quick Reference
reference.trumpStrength:
Trump Strength Order
Tra(5) > Heart(4) > Diamond(3) > Club(2) > Spade(1)
reference.bonus:
Strength Bonus
Trump Suit: +100 / Base Suit: +50
reference.jackSystem:
Jack System
Main J (Jack of the trump suit) / Sub J (varies by trump)
reference.scoreCalc:
Score Calculation
Y≥X: 0.5×(Y-X)+X-5 points / Y'<'X: Y-X points
(X=declared pairs, Y=fields won)
reference.violations:
Chombo [Not Implemented]
Violation rules are planned for a future update
reference.flow:
Game Flow
Deal -> Bid -> Trick (until 10 fields are complete)
reference.settings:
Basic Settings
4 players, 2 teams / Opposite seat = partner / Target score is configurable per room / Minimum bid is 6 pairs

section1.title: Article 1 (Application)
section1.p1: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern the use of the Mei-Tra online card game service ("Service"). All users ("Users") who use the Service are deemed to have agreed to these Terms.
section1.p2: The Service is a web application that allows users to play "Mei-Tra," a 4-player, 2-team card game, online.

section2.title: Article 2 (Registration)
section2.p1: To use the Service, you must create an account by registering your email address, password, username, and display name. The information you provide must be accurate and up to date.
section2.p2: The operator may suspend or delete accounts that are found to contain false information or to have been registered improperly.

section3.title: Article 3 (Prohibited Conduct)
section3.item1: Defamation, harassment, or threats toward other users
section3.item2: Cheating, unfair game manipulation, or unauthorized access to systems
section3.item3: Conduct that interferes with the operation of the Service
section3.item5: Conduct that violates laws or public order and morals
section3.item6: Any other conduct deemed inappropriate by the operator

section4.title: Article 4 (Account Management)
section4.p1: Users are responsible for managing their own account information. The operator is not liable for any damage resulting from password leakage or unauthorized use by third parties.
section4.p2: Transfer, lending, or sale of accounts is prohibited.

section5.title: Article 5 (Disclaimer)
section5.p1: The Service is provided as is. The operator does not warrant the accuracy, completeness, or usefulness of the Service. Game results, score records, and other data may be lost due to technical issues, and the operator is not liable for such losses.

section6.title: Article 6 (Service Changes and Termination)
section6.p1: The operator may modify, temporarily suspend, or terminate the Service without prior notice.
section6.p2: The operator is not liable for any damage to users resulting from such changes.

section7.title: Article 7 (Governing Law and Jurisdiction)
section7.p1: These Terms are governed by the laws of Japan. Any disputes arising from the Service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the court with jurisdiction over the operator's principal place of business.

section8.title: Article 8 (Amendment of Terms)
section8.p1: The operator may amend these Terms as necessary. Amended Terms take effect upon posting on the Service. Use of the Service after amendment constitutes acceptance of the amended Terms.

section1.title: 1. Introduction
section1.p1: The Mei-Tra online card game service ("Service") values the protection of users' personal information. This Privacy Policy explains the information we collect and use through the Service.
section1.p2: By using the Service, you are deemed to have agreed to this Policy.

section2.title: 2. Information We Collect
section2.p1: The Service collects and uses the following information.
section2.item1: Account information: Email address, password (stored encrypted), username, display name
section2.item2: Game usage data: Room participation history, match results, chat messages
section2.item3: Technical data: IP address, browser type, device information, connection logs

section3.title: 3. Purpose of Use
section3.p1: Collected information is used for providing and operating the Service, user authentication, preventing misuse, service improvement, and responding to inquiries.
section3.p2: In-game chat and display names are visible to other players in the same room.

section4.title: 4. Third-Party Sharing
section4.p1: The Service uses Supabase (Supabase Inc.) for authentication, database, and hosting. These service providers process information only for the purpose of providing the Service.
section4.p2: We do not share personal information with third parties without user consent, except when required by law.

section5.title: 5. Data Retention and Deletion
section5.p1: When an account is deleted or after a period of inactivity, we may delete associated data. Deleted data cannot be recovered.

section6.title: 6. Contact
section6.p1: For inquiries regarding this Policy, please contact the operating company at https://kando1.com.
